en.extract:
The grey-headed lapwing (Vanellus cinereus) is a lapwing species which breeds in northeast China and Japan. The mainland population winters in northern Southeast Asia from northeastern India to Cambodia.  The Japanese population winters, at least partially, in southern Honshū.

This species has occurred as a vagrant in Russia, the Philippines, Indonesia, New South Wales, Australia, Telangana  and  Sri Lanka, as well as Sweden and England.


Description
The grey-headed lapwing is 34–37 cm long. It has a grey head and neck, darker grey breast band and white belly. The back is brown, the rump is white and the tail is black. This is a striking species in flight, with black primaries, white under wings and upper wing secondaries, and brown upper wing coverts.
Adults of both sexes are similarly plumaged, but males are slightly larger than females. Young birds have the white areas of plumage tinged with grey, a less distinct breast band, and pale fringes to the upperpart and wing covert feathers. The call of the grey-headed lapwing is a sharp chee-it.


Behaviour
This species nests from April to July in wet grassland, rice fields and marshland edges. It winters in similar habitat and is then gregarious. It feeds in shallow water on insects, worms and molluscs.


References

Hayman, Marchant and Prater, Shorebirds ISBN 0-395-37903-2
Robson, Craig  A Field Guide to the Birds of Thailand ISBN 1-84330-921-1

''Pluvianus cinereus'' Blyth, 1842 }} The '''grey-headed lapwing''' ('''''Vanellus cinereus''''') is a [[lapwing]] species which breeds in [[northeast China]] and [[Japan]]. The mainland population [[bird migration|winters]] in northern [[Southeast Asia]] from northeastern [[India]] to [[Cambodia]]. The Japanese population winters, at least partially, in southern [[Honshū]]. [[File:Grey-headed Lapwing, Kirtipur, Kathmandu, Nepal.jpg|thumb|left|In flight at [[Kathmandu Valley]], [[Nepal]].]] This species has occurred as a vagrant in [[Russia]], the [[Philippines]], [[Indonesia]], [[New South Wales]], [[Australia]], [[Telangana]]{{Cite web |last1=Sethy |first1=Itishree |title= Rare Sighting of the Grey-headed Lapwing in Telangana After Many Years|work=Pragativadi |date=28 January 2025 |url=https://pragativadi.com/rare-sighting-of-the-grey-headed-lapwing-in-telangana-after-many-years/}} and [[Sri Lanka]], as well as [[Sweden]] and [[England]].{{Cite news |date=2023-05-02 |title=Grey-headed lapwing: Sighting in Northumberland is UK first |language=en-GB |work=BBC News |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-england-tyne-65456945 |access-date=2023-06-20}} ==Description== The grey-headed lapwing is 34–37 cm long. It has a grey head and neck, darker grey breast band and white belly. The back is brown, the rump is white and the tail is black. This is a striking species in flight, with black primaries, white under wings and upper wing secondaries, and brown upper wing coverts. Adults of both sexes are similarly plumaged, but males are slightly larger than females. Young birds have the white areas of plumage tinged with grey, a less distinct breast band, and pale fringes to the upperpart and wing covert feathers. The call of the grey-headed lapwing is a sharp ''chee-it''. [[File:2023-05-01 Grey-headed Lapwing, Low Newton, Northumberland 1.jpg|thumb|left|The first grey-headed lapwing seen in Britain was on 1 May 2023 at [[Newton-by-the-Sea]] in [[Northumberland]].]] ==Behaviour== This species nests from April to July in wet grassland, [[rice|rice fields]] and marshland edges. It winters in similar habitat and is then gregarious. It feeds in shallow water on [[insect]]s, [[worm]]s and [[mollusc]]s. ==References== {{Commons category|Vanellus cinereus}} {{Wikispecies|Vanellus cinereus}} {{Reflist}} *Hayman, Marchant and Prater, ''Shorebirds'' {{ISBN|0-395-37903-2}} *Robson, Craig ''A Field Guide to the Birds of Thailand'' {{ISBN|1-84330-921-1}} {{Taxonbar|from=Q1047787}} [[Category:Vanellus]] [[Category:Birds of Japan]] [[Category:Birds of Manchuria]] [[Category:Wintering birds of Indochina]] [[Category:Articles containing video clips]] [[Category:Birds described in 1842]] [[Category:Taxa named by Edward Blyth]]

zh.extract:
灰头麦鸡（学名：Vanellus cinereus）-为鸻科麦鸡属的鸟类。主要繁殖於中國東北和日本。大陸的族群在東南亞北部過冬，從印度東北部到柬埔寨。而日本的族群至少部分會在本州南部過冬。在中国大陆，分布于华北、华南等地。该物种的模式产地在印度。

此物種曾作為迷鳥出現在俄羅斯、菲律賓、印尼、新南威爾士、澳洲和斯里蘭卡， 也有在瑞典和英格蘭的記錄。


描述
跳鴴體長約34至37公分。牠們具有灰色的頭部和頸部、較深的灰色胸帶和白色的腹部。背部呈棕色，臀部為白色，尾巴則為黑色。當飛行時，牠們是非常顯眼的物種，擁有黑色的初級飛羽、白色的下翼和上翼次級飛羽，以及棕色的上翼覆羽。
兩性成鳥的羽毛顏色相似，但雄鳥稍大於雌鳥。幼鳥的白色羽毛帶有灰色，胸帶不太明顯，上體及翼覆羽羽毛具有淺色邊緣。跳鴴的叫聲尖銳，呈現chee-it的聲音。


行為
這個物種在4月至7月間於濕草地、稻田和沼澤邊緣築巢。牠們在類似的棲地過冬，並且在此時具有群聚性。牠們在淺水中覓食，主要以昆蟲、蠕蟲和軟體動物為食。
牠們主要繁殖於中國東北和日本，每年秋冬時節會南遷到中南半島一帶度冬。
